description: Trigger-level tests: does the skill fire (or correctly not fire) for a given prompt? No execution required — evaluated against the SKILL.md description only, the way Claude's skill router would.

evals.query: Use the linkedin-content-generator skill on my deployment pipeline notes and give me the full content package.
evals.note: Explicit, names the skill directly. Baseline sanity check.

evals.query: Turn this attached writeup on our infra decision framework into a LinkedIn article and posts.
evals.note: Explicit LinkedIn + Article + posts language with an attached source.

evals.query: I just finished documenting how we structured the agent orchestration layer for our multi-agent system. Can you help me get this in front of CTOs and technical founders on LinkedIn?
evals.note: No mention of 'skill', 'article', or 'post' by name -- has to be inferred from intent + audience language matching the brand positioning.

evals.query: here's my notes from building a scaling framework for our deployment system (pasted below). make something out of this i can share to build my profile as an ai systems builder
evals.note: Casual phrasing, lowercase, no explicit platform named but 'profile' + brand language + pasted source material should be enough.

evals.query: can you turn my notes into a post
evals.note: REVISED after description update: no platform named, no 'share professionally'/'build my profile' signal -- description now explicitly excludes a platform-less 'make this a post.' Correctly should NOT trigger; if the user wants LinkedIn specifically they need to say so or it needs to be established earlier in the conversation.

evals.query: What's a good commenting strategy to build engagement on other people's LinkedIn posts in the AI space?
evals.note: Explicitly out of scope per the skill's own negative trigger -- commenting strategy.

evals.query: Write me a connection request message to send to a VP of Engineering at an AI startup.
Write me a LinkedIn post about why agentic AI is going to change software engineering.
evals.note: No source material identifiable anywhere in the prompt -- this is asking Claude to originate the idea, which the skill must refuse to do. Borderline: skill should not silently trigger-and-comply; either it doesn't trigger, or it triggers and immediately asks for source material rather than drafting. Flag for human judgment either way.

evals.query: Can you review my LinkedIn About section and headline and suggest improvements?
evals.note: Adjacent (LinkedIn, profile positioning) but not article/post generation from source material -- near-miss test.

evals.query: Attached is a transcript of a talk I gave on our RAG pipeline failures. Turn it into LinkedIn content.
evals.note: Real attached source material, LinkedIn content requested -- squarely in scope.

evals.query: Attached is a transcript of a talk I gave on our RAG pipeline failures. Can you write me a Twitter/X thread version of this?
evals.note: Same kind of source + same underlying task shape, but a different platform than this skill covers. Near-miss: shares 'turn source into social content' but LinkedIn-specific formatting/brand rules don't apply.

evals.query: Here are my system design notes -- can you turn these into a formal Word document report for my manager?
evals.note: Source material + Word doc output (surface overlap with this skill's deliverable format) but not LinkedIn content at all -- should route to the docx skill instead, not this one.

evals.query: I want to grow my LinkedIn following fast, what's the best posting cadence and hashtag strategy in general?
evals.note: General LinkedIn growth/algorithm question, no source material, not asking for a drafted piece of content -- outside scope.

evals.query: Just the article, not a full package -- turn my attached notes on our evaluation framework into a single long-form LinkedIn article.
evals.note: Should be classified as Article-only per Step 2's table, not escalate to full-package confirmation.

evals.query: From this changelog I pasted below, just give me one short LinkedIn post about the caching bug we fixed. No article needed.
evals.note: Explicit single-post scope, should not trigger full-package confirmation checkpoint.

evals.query: Take these system design and deployment notes and build out a full LinkedIn content package for me.
evals.note: 'full content package' is explicit; should trigger the Step 2 confirmation checkpoint before executing.

evals.query: Here's my writeup on our new agent memory architecture, do something with this for LinkedIn.
evals.note: Scope not specified -- per the skill's classification table this defaults toward full package, but is vague enough it's reasonable to confirm scope with the user first rather than assume.
